_README: Copy this to accounts.local.json (gitignored) and fill in secrets — OR paste them in the
Account Pool UI (:4577 → 🔑 Account Pool tab), which writes this same file for you.
It is merged BY id onto config.json -> providers[*].accounts[*] at load time.
(1) login account   -> { id, configDir }       a pre-logged-in CLI config dir; NO secret here.
The engine sets env[accountEnv]=configDir per dispatch. Log in ONCE per
dir; the CLI keeps a refresh token that auto-renews — that is what makes
'login once, lasts long' work (no proxy/VM needed).
(2) apiKey account  -> { id, envKey, apiKey }   the key is set as env[envKey] before spawn.

codex._setup: 3 ChatGPT-plan seats as login dirs. Run each once: `CODEX_HOME=~/.codex-1 codex login` (then -2, -3), or click 'login' in the Account Pool UI. configDir lives in config.json; nothing secret needed here.

claude._setup: 1 seat. Default dir ~/.claude is already your logged-in Claude CLI — no action needed unless you want an isolated dir.

antigravity._setup: agy stores its interactive login in the OS keyring (Windows Credential Manager) = ONE slot per OS user, so config-dir swap does NOT give 5 concurrent seats. The only per-account path on one box is ANTIGRAVITY_TOKEN (the CI/non-interactive token). Paste one token per seat below. OPEN RISK: verify you can obtain 5 tokens for subscription accounts; if not, only one agy seat is usable at a time.
